steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined portion is an em-dash clause immediately after "convergent evolution": "a phenomenon that occurs when the same trait evolves independently in two reproductively separate lineages" The question asks what role this clause plays in the passage as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It clarifies a concept that the author implies was unclear in the studies mentioned in the text.: The text never suggests the concept was unclear to the scientists involved. The definition is for the reader's benefit, not a correction of any confusion. ✗
  • Choice B — It provides examples of how a phenomenon was studied by scientists in the field before Brooks and Shah's study.: The underlined portion is a definition, not examples of study methods or prior research approaches. ✗
  • Choice C — It introduces a method of scientific analysis that is discussed in greater detail later in the text.: The clause defines a phenomenon, not a method. No method is elaborated further in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — It gives a basic description of a phenomenon that is central to the discussion that follows.: The clause defines convergent evolution in plain terms, and the entire passage — Lin's research, Kim's research, and Brooks and Shah's study — all revolve around this phenomenon. ✓
That matches Choice D — It gives a basic description of a phenomenon that is central to the discussion that follows..
